f_star.note: transition from steep rise (slope ~20 at 22 Hz) to plateau (slope ~0.02 at 500 Hz)

N_500.note: single dominant mode at 1% threshold; second mode marginally significant at 0.1% threshold

T2_ridge_angle.method: angle between leading Fisher eigenvector v₁(f) and displacement vector vd at each frequency
T2_ridge_angle.note: v₁ lives in ln M_c subspace (99.97%); vd lives in Λ̃ subspace (99.88%). They are essentially orthogonal at all frequencies, meaning no overlap between the dominant Fisher direction and the posterior displacement direction. This geometrically explains why T3 fails to find alignment — the test compares a displacement in tidal-deformability space against an eigenvector that doesn't even point into that dimension.

slopes.note: low-frequency slope is ~20 (steep rise), high-frequency slope flattens to ~0.02
